changes.before: Cold climbed into her palm at once. Not her own clean cold. This one was wet, patient, greedy, sunk through with the same buried drag Hearthglen's stores had carried before the dead rose inside the walls.
changes.after: Cold climbed into her palm at once, wet and patient and greedy, sunk through with the same buried drag she had felt in Hearthglen's stores before the dead rose inside the walls.
This store was filthier to break.

changes.before: A nest of them, each trying to stay tied to stock already on wheels and in kitchens and on hands.
changes.after: A nest of them, each still tied to stock already on wheels and in kitchens and on hands.

changes.before: The line had crossed from stock to bodies, from bodies to households,
changes.after: Bread was already in hands, houses, and bodies.

changes.before: from households to a city that still thought it had time to argue over prices and get children fed before noon.
changes.after: Bread was already in hands, houses, and bodies.

changes.before: It would not pull a single mouthful back out of the bodies already carrying the city toward its death.
The line had crossed from stock to bodies, from bodies to households, from households to a city that still thought it had time to argue over prices and get children fed before noon.
changes.after: Bread was already in hands, houses, and bodies.

changes.before: The southern gate was Stratholme's formal face: high arch, worked iron, stone broad enough to flatter a prince riding under it.
changes.after: The southern gate was the formal one: high arch, worked iron, stone broad enough for a prince to ride under it.

changes.before: The line had crossed from stock to bodies, from bodies to households, from households to a city that still thought it had time to argue over prices and get children fed before noon.
changes.after: Bread was already in hands, houses, and bodies.
